description: ADR-047 follow-up — three items the parent workplan (wp-adr-047-docs-phases-2-5) has reconciler-flagged as done but ground-truth grep proves are not yet shipped. Filed as a fresh workplan rather than fighting the auto-reconciler on the parent. Phases 1-2-3-4.1-5.1(tooling)-5.3 already shipped in commits 15841351, 5612efe6, 65488f6b, 81a03edc.

phases.description: Implement the pre-commit warning for stale spacetime-module READMEs (parent workplan P4.2). Real path is hex-cli/src/commands/hook/mod.rs, not the now-stale hook.rs the parent workplan cites.
phases.name: Pre-commit warning for stale module READMEs

tasks.description: Add a pre-commit hook entry under hex-cli/src/commands/hook/ that, given a list of files staged for commit, warns when any file under spacetime-modules/<m>/ has changed but spacetime-modules/<m>/README.md has not. Wire it into the existing hex hook command surface. Source: ADR-047 §Documentation Freshness item 2.
tasks.name: Implement stale-module-readme pre-commit warning

phases.description: Implement the hex adr graph subcommand and cascading staleness for hex adr abandoned (parent workplan P5.2). Real path is hex-cli/src/commands/adr/mod.rs (now a module dir, not adr.rs).
phases.name: hex adr graph + cascading staleness

tasks.description: Add the `hex adr graph` subcommand. Reads YAML frontmatter from every ADR in docs/adrs/ (skips ADRs without frontmatter — those still emit `missing_adr_frontmatter` warnings via hex docs check). Builds depends_on / supersedes / superseded_by graph. Emits mermaid by default; --json for tooling. Then extend hex adr abandoned to walk depends_on chains so cascading staleness is detected. Source: ADR-047 §ADR staleness + §ADR dependency graph.
tasks.name: Implement hex adr graph + cascading abandoned

phases.description: Bulk-migrate the remaining ADRs to the YAML frontmatter spec defined in ADR-047 §Tier 2. ADR-047 itself is already migrated (commit 81a03edc). Tooling already shipped: hex docs migrate-adr <id> --apply (idempotent). hex docs check currently reports ~190 missing_adr_frontmatter warnings — that count is the success metric.

gate.command: ./target/release/hex docs check --json 2>/dev/null | jq '[.findings[] | select(.kind == "missing_adr_frontmatter")] | length' | grep -qE '^(0|[1-9])$' && [ "$(./target/release/hex docs check --json 2>/dev/null | jq '[.findings[] | select(.kind == "missing_adr_frontmatter")] | length')" -lt 5 ]

phases.name: Bulk ADR frontmatter migration

tasks.description: Run `hex docs migrate-adr <id> --apply` against every ADR currently flagged by `hex docs check` with kind=missing_adr_frontmatter. Spot-check 5 random ADRs to verify components/modules fields were sensibly inferred. Re-run hex docs check at the end and confirm the missing_adr_frontmatter count drops to <5 (allowing for unparseable edge cases, which should each get a brief comment in this task).
tasks.name: Run migrate-adr across all flagged ADRs
